text.title: monorepo-turborepo Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for monorepo-turborepo as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Applying tsup build to Next.js app packages instead of libraries.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Next.js manages its own bundling; using tsup on an app package breaks the Next.js build pipeline and duplicate bundling.

failure-mode.description: Circular workspace dependencies: packages/a depends on packages/b which depends on packages/a.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Turborepo cannot build a task graph with a cycle; the build hangs or fails with a cryptic error. Extract shared code into a third packages/shared with no deps.

failure-mode.description: Missing dependsOn on typecheck or lint tasks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without dependsOn: ["^build"], Turborepo may run typecheck before the upstream build emits declaration files, producing false type errors.

failure-mode.description: Adding dev server to the cache with outputs: [].
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Dev servers are non-deterministic; caching them causes Turborepo to serve a stale server on next run. Set cache: false, persistent: true instead.

failure-mode.description: Agent edits pnpm-lock.yaml by hand to resolve a merge conflict.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Manual edits corrupt integrity hashes; always re-run pnpm install to regenerate the lockfile.

failure-mode.description: Agent uses --filter=@monorepo/web... (three dots) thinking it selects the package and its dependencies.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Three trailing dots means "package and its dependents" (reverse deps), not dependencies. Use ^... for dependencies, ... for dependents.

failure-mode.description: Agent introduces dependency cycles when refactoring: @monorepo/ui → @monorepo/utils → @monorepo/ui.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Run manypkg check and madge --circular post-edit to catch cycles before they reach CI.

failure-mode.description: Adding a new task: agent forgets to declare outputs → turbo caches nothing, runs every time.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always specify outputs even if [] for "side-effect-only" tasks so Turborepo knows what to cache.

failure-mode.description: Agent puts TURBO_TOKEN in repo vars instead of secrets.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Token in vars is readable by any workflow; always use GitHub Secrets.

failure-mode.description: Persistent dev tasks under Turborepo: agent runs turbo dev then issues Ctrl+C from the LLM via timeout — leaves orphan processes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use --ui=stream and explicit pkill -f turbo cleanup instead.
